articles.title: STADLER reshapes knowledge work at a 230-year-old company
articles.summary: STADLER demonstrates how ChatGPT transforms knowledge work across a traditional manufacturing company with 650 employees. The case study shows significant time savings and productivity acceleration in enterprise workflows.

articles.title: Why OpenAI killed Sora
articles.summary: OpenAI announced the sudden cancellation of its Sora video-generation app and reversed ChatGPT video plans. The company also wound down a $1 billion Disney deal while raising an additional $10 billion, bringing total funding to over $120 billion as it rushes to achieve profitability.

articles.title: Why SoftBank's new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
articles.summary: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are providing SoftBank with a 12-month unsecured loan. This financial move suggests strategic positioning for an anticipated OpenAI IPO in 2026.

articles.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end 'RAMmageddon' with blockbuster US IPO
articles.summary: SK hynix is considering a U.S. IPO that could raise $10-14 billion to build more memory capacity. The move could help alleviate the ongoing memory chip shortage dubbed 'RAMmageddon' and encourage other manufacturers to expand production.

articles.title: With new plugins feature, OpenAI officially takes Codex beyond coding
articles.summary: OpenAI has added plugins functionality to Codex, expanding its capabilities beyond pure coding tasks. This move helps close the feature gap with competing AI coding tools like Claude Code.

articles.title: Sony temporarily suspends memory card sales due to shortages
articles.summary: Sony has stopped accepting orders for most CFexpress and SD memory cards as of March 27th, 2026. The suspension affects CFexpress Type A, Type B, and SDXC/SDHC cards, with only a few low-end models remaining in production.

articles.title: Oppo made the best foldable phone, again
articles.summary: The Oppo Find N6 addresses the fundamental issue of foldable phones with a nearly invisible crease. When closed, it maintains the thinness of regular phones while solving the durability and design problems that have plagued foldables.

articles.title: A woman's uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
articles.summary: Scientists have achieved a medical breakthrough by keeping a human uterus alive outside the body for the first time. The system uses a metal box with plastic tubing that acts as veins and arteries to maintain the organ.

articles.title: Wait, the Trump phone might actually exist
articles.summary: An FCC listing suggests Trump Mobile's T1 Phone has received certification to launch in the US. This development indicates the previously speculative device may actually become a reality.

articles.title: Vibe coding SwiftUI apps is a lot of fun
articles.summary: Simon Willison shares his experience with SwiftUI development, highlighting the enjoyable aspects of building apps with Apple's declarative UI framework.

articles.title: datasette-showboat 0.1a2
articles.summary: Release announcement for datasette-showboat version 0.1a2, a plugin or tool in the Datasette ecosystem for data exploration and visualization.
